caption:
The power isn’t in the information… it’s in the room.

Inner Circle isn’t just a mastermind… it’s where ideas collide, strategies sharpen, and real relationships are built.

When you get this many high-level entrepreneurs in one place—people who are actually in it, building, testing, scaling—something shifts.

Today, Russell Brunson pulled back the curtain… shared next-level strategies, and gave us a glimpse at what’s coming next 👀
(Inner Circle always hears it first.)

This is the kind of room where one conversation can change everything.

Grateful to be in it. Grateful to learn from it. Grateful to build alongside it.

Inner Circle for life.

#InnerCircle #FunnelHacker #MastermindMagic

caption:
Virality usually isn’t luck.

It’s repetition. 

The people who win test their ideas on small stages…
so when the big stage comes, the message is already proven.
firstComment:
Virality is nice 🤩

But without trust, structure and aligned messaging… it’s just attention, not conversion 💸

That’s the part most people miss! 👀

latestComments.text:
Virality is nice 🤩

But without trust, structure and aligned messaging… it’s just attention, not conversion 💸

That’s the part most people miss! 👀
This makes a lot of sense 👏
This is such a powerful reminder that consistency and testing matter more than chasing viral moments. Repetition really is what builds messages that stick and perform.

Repetition is how every winning offer in our group got built. Tested the same pitch in small rooms for months before putting any real budget behind it. The version that scaled was never the first version. But most people only try once and assume the market said no.

@russellbrunson thank you for all your do. I am based in Tanzania. I have read all your books,  attended several of your events, bought and using click funnel. You ve been mentoring me and I absolutely love your content

caption:
You’ve been called to serve. 

Your job is not to convert the world of your way of thinking…
Your job is to get your message out there and I promise, your people are going to hear you.

This is way more than just making money… this is your chance to change people’s lives with your stories and unique perspectives and ideas. 

We’ve all got insanely valuable stories and experiences to share with each other. 

Shift your focus… and start putting your message out there to HELP others. 🧠

#helpothersgrow #tellyourstory #entrepreneurminds #entrepreneurinspiration #lifteachotherup #calledtoserve
firstComment: Hello from Mexico Rusell, which should be my firts thought if i want to serve someone?

latestComments.text: Hello from Mexico Rusell, which should be my firts thought if i want to serve someone?
Entrepreneurship is taking responsibility for outcomes most people avoid where your income reflects how well you solve problems and stay consistent through uncertainty

Took me years to understand this properly. The businesses in the portfolio that are growing fastest right now are the ones where we stopped asking what can we sell and started asking what do these people actually need. Sounds obvious but the shift in how you operate day to day is massive. Service first, money follows. Every time.
I like this. Focusing on helping people first often creates stronger, more sustainable outcomes over time.

caption:
Pick the market.
Test the passion.
Confirm they spend.

That’s how you avoid wasting years in the wrong niche.

Comment EXPERT and I’ll send you the link to order your book!

latestComments.text: This is such a valuable lesson—and honestly one a lot of founders only learn the hard way 👏
Need to think more on this
Most people pick the market and skip straight to building. Testing the passion and confirming spend first saves years of wasted effort. Simple framework, massive impact.

Ambitious dads over 40 killing it in business but losing in their body. Passionate because I was that guy. And they spend when the solution speaks to their identity as a father. Niche confirmed the hard way. I had to become the proof first.

Wasted 18 months on a business in the wrong market before I learned this. Great product, real demand, but the audience couldn't afford the price point we needed to make the margins work. Moved the same core offer to a different market and it was profitable in 3 months. Market selection is the decision that everything else depends on.
Get clear, test it, then go all in - let the market guide you! EXPERT 🙌🏼

caption:
Comment “MINDS” and I’ll DM you the full video… you NEED to see this before 2025 ends.

By the 1980s, Dan Kennedy wasn’t just teaching marketing…
He was training renegade millionaires to hijack the mind.

At the center of it all was one disturbing realization:
The better the mind control, the higher the price.

caption:
The owner of OnlyFans made $701 million in a single year, while the average creator makes just $180 a month. Yet, the media is calling him a "pioneer" and a "visionary."

How did a platform built on explicit content convince millions of young women that it was "empowerment"? It’s the exact same 100-year-old propaganda trick Edward Bernays used to make women smoke cigarettes by calling them "torches of freedom."

Same technique. Same target. Same lie. Different century.

:red_circle: Watch the full breakdown here: https://youtu.be/Q_j45oREr-U

#onlyfans #propaganda #propagandaplaybook #russellbrunson #marketing #creator #business

caption:
"The problem with launch-based businesses?

You can win big one year…
and be holding your breath the next.

Predictable revenue changes everything."
firstComment: Predictable revenue is where real scaling begins!

latestComments.text: Predictable revenue is where real scaling begins!

Predictable revenue is the whole game. Had businesses doing great numbers on launches and couldn't sleep because I never knew if the next one would land. Moved everything to recurring models across the portfolio and the stress dropped overnight. Revenue went up too which was the surprise.

caption:
The secret to a great presentation isn’t just what you say.

It’s trial closes.

Instead of talking at the audience, you keep pulling them in with questions:

“Does that make sense?”
“Can you see how this would work for you?”
“What would you do in this situation?”

These small questions get people thinking, nodding, and agreeing as you go.

Because when someone has already said “yes” in their mind 10 times…

The final yes becomes easy.

latestComments.text: The best presenters know that nodding heads convert faster than open mouths ever will.

Exactly! 
Engagement isn’t about talking louder, it’s about asking the right questions. 

Get them nodding along early, and the final yes comes naturally. 💪🔥
Engagement beats perfection - get people involved, and the sale becomes the natural next step!
Trial closes work in parenting too. I use the same principle training with my son. Small wins stack until saying yes to the hard stuff becomes automatic. 13 years of proof.
Every “yes” before the pitch makes the final yes inevitable @russellbrunson

caption:
@drjoevitale sent me an eBay listing and said, “This belongs in your library.”

At first I thought it was the weirdest thing ever… until I read what it actually was.

A 1920s psycho-phone, a device designed to play positive messages to your subconscious while you slept.

The idea was simple:
Set it to play during your sleep cycle so it could whisper suggestions to your subconscious… helping you wake up feeling smarter, more confident, and more capable.

Basically… personal development technology from 100 years ago.

Had to add this one to the collection. Pretty cool piece of history.

latestComments.text: Love that you collect stuff like this. There's something about holding a physical piece of history that hits differently than reading about it online. The psycho-phone concept is basically what half the personal development apps are doing now just with worse branding. 100 years ahead of its time honestly.
While everyone's doom scrolling before bed, Russell's out here collecting vintage brain hacking gear that's literally older than social media by a century.
That’s pretty sick dude!! Reminds me of “the secrets” pillows lol

caption:
What’s up, guys! We have a huge event happening tomorrow in the Inner Circle room—Two CCX are coming in, and I wanted to give you a sneak peek.

I decided to maximize every corner of this event room for some seriously cool stuff. If you remember from the One Comma Club Challenge, we built out a podcast/challenge studio in one corner, which has been awesome for business. But we still had two empty corners… until now!

Check this out: I’m officially starting an LDS Mormon podcast, and we just set up the new backdrop today. It features Joseph Smith and the First Vision, and I am beyond excited about it.

Coming soon to a podcast near you! 🎙️✨

alt: Photo by Russell Brunson on March 20, 2026. May be an image of shadow box, picture frame and text.

caption:
Exactly a year ago this week, during a wrestling tournament I tore both of my biceps. Not strained them, literally the bicep ripped off of the bone on both arms! 

Yes it was in two separate matches… 

Yes I kept wrestling after the first one tore off… 

…and yes I did beat the guy even though both my biceps were gone!

What came next was a very long season of surgeries and rehab and PT and me trying to heal as fast as humanly possible, hitting it from every direction, from acupuncture to stem cells to lasers to everything you could dream of to make me heal faster. 

I did it and now 12 months later I'm very happy to announce that I'm doing great. I'm lifting heavier now than I was prior to the bicep surgeries and just starting to get back into training and wrestling once again. 

You probably saw me wrestling last week at the UFC training center with some of the UFC fighters. That was my first actual practice since the double bicep surgery… and It felt amazing! 

I can't wait to see what's coming next and to compete in some tournaments later on this year!  Thanks to all the doctors, coaches, trainers and friends who helped me to heal faster!!

…and special thanks to @cocalettie for taking care of me when I was down.  You’re the best! I love you!!

latestComments.text: That’s 12 months lost doing what you love .. Tap early and stay in the game longer. Glad you’re recovered and able to keep training.

Using test weakens your collagen levels and makes for easier injuries. 
Stay safe out there with the gear!
Must have been one hell of a throw to tear a bicep. Greco or freestyle?
I feel you man🙌 Just have my arm brace and swesing
Would love to know which healing  methods you tried that you would recommend to others.

That’s next-level resilience.

Most would’ve stopped at the first setback, you pushed through both, rebuilt, and came back stronger. 
That’s not luck, that’s mindset.

Respect the grind. The comeback is earned. 💪🔥

caption:
Entrepreneurship starts with getting comfortable hearing no.

 @kristinemirelle sold thousands of albums door to door at 17.

9 out of 10 people said no.
Doors slammed.
People telling her to get a real job.

But 1 out of 10 said yes.

And that’s the math most entrepreneurs eventually learn:

You don’t need everyone.
You just need the right ones.
firstComment: This is a good reminder that we need to be okay with lots of no's before we get the "YES"!

latestComments.text: This is a good reminder that we need to be okay with lots of no's before we get the "YES"!
We can thank those 9 people one rejection at a time because each one that says no, gets us closer to the yes.
It’s a math problem, not a confidence problem.
This is a good reminder that we need to be okay with lots of nos before we get the yeses

caption:
Hey, what’s up! Check this out—I’m currently at the lot where we are building the brand new Event Center / Museum, right next to ClickFunnels HQ here in Boise.

They’ve been digging it out and getting ready for the foundations. This spot right here is where the huge stage will be, and behind it, we have an event room that will hold about 300 people. Boom! This is where millions of lives are going to be changed.

It’s a really big building! When you walk down to where the stone is, that’s the main entrance where you’ll be greeted by three Atlas statues. On the other side is where the museum is going to be—filled with all the old books, manuscripts, and cool nerdy stuff I’ve been investing in over the last couple of years.

Two full floors of awesome stuff. It’s a little bit ridiculous and amazing at the same time. I am so excited about this and I’ll keep you guys posted on the progress! 🏗️📚✨

#ClickFunnels #ClickFunnelsHQ #BoiseIdaho #EventCenter #MuseumBuild BehindTheScenes EntrepreneurLife ChangingLives AtlasStatues RareBooks Manuscripts ComingSoon

latestComments.text: Very cool Russell!  What an incredible space this is going to be. ❤️
The vision is dope.  I love your imagination on how to do the layout.  Always hard to design something like this... unless you're Russell Brunson! :)
So cool can’t wait to come and visit ❤️
😍 WOW so much cool game changer

caption:
What’s your superpower?

The thing that feels easy to you…
The thing people always come to you for help with…

That’s usually the clue.
firstComment: The best marketing I've ever done was simply telling the truth about what happened in my businesses. A case study where a client went from £17k to £110k a month converts ten times better than any scripted pitch. Real stories build trust. Trust converts. Everything else is noise.

latestComments.text: The best marketing I've ever done was simply telling the truth about what happened in my businesses. A case study where a client went from £17k to £110k a month converts ten times better than any scripted pitch. Real stories build trust. Trust converts. Everything else is noise.

The idea that our "superpower" feels like second nature to us is exactly why so many people overlook their best business ideas. We tend to undervalue what comes easy, but that’s usually where the actual leverage is for everyone else..... Great share, I remember Russell teaching this years ago on the OFA
This makes a lot of sense 👏
Your superpowers are the gifts,talents and abilities you are given to share. Where your personal responsibility is to refine them and share them with the world. Well done Russell!

caption:
So some of you may already know this… but right next to our headquarters we’re building something really special.

A library that will house the largest Success magazine collection in the world… and an event space where we can bring people together to learn, share ideas, and keep this legacy alive.

Designing this building has been one of the coolest experiences ever. Recently, I was going through wood samples trying to get every detail just right.

I seriously cannot wait for this building to be finished.

latestComments.text: This is inspiring. Not just building success, but building a home for it where people can gather, grow, and pass it forward.
🔥🔥🔥 I can’t wait to see it!
Woohoo!!! Can’t wait to see it when it’s done! 🔥❤️
Love that you’re creating a space for learning and community.
Love this! We need to preserve these books 📕

alt: Photo by Russell Brunson on March 16, 2026. May be an image of studying, phone, lego, office and text.

caption:
Recently I went to Charlotte for the Aspire event.
Even better getting to do it with @billallenrei and @bcoppieters by my side. These guys are the real deal and it’s always more fun when you get to share the journey with great people. Grateful for friends like these.

latestComments.text: Nothing beats learning and growing alongside people you respect.
Nothing beats building an empire with people who actually get it and won't slow you down.

The people you surround yourself with at events like Aspire make all the difference. Good friends who are also serious about growth push you further than you can push yourself alone. Building those relationships online extends that circle beyond geography - and tools like Crescitaly.com help you grow your social media audience so you attract more of those like-minded people into your world. Grateful for real ones!
I had a podcast with Bill, later on he gifted me his signed book. Amazing guy. 👏👏👏

The people you surround yourself with at events like this matter more than the content itself. Half the breakthroughs in my businesses came from conversations at dinners after the main event, not from the stage. Good circle makes all the difference.
